channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tammy Tyrrell officially joins the Labor Party after previously criticising it
item.description: Tammy Tyrrell, previously an independent Senator known for her critical remarks about the Labor Party, has officially joined the party, marking a significant shift in her political stance. Over the pa...

item.title: David Beckham reaches billionaire status as first UK sportsperson
item.description: David Beckham, former England football captain and global sports icon, has officially become the first British sportsperson to reach billionaire status. His earnings over the years have come not just ...

item.title: 2009 Court Records Show Trump Listed in Epstein-Related Lawsuit
item.description: Newly discovered court documents from 2009 reveal that a woman who survived abuse by Jeffrey Epstein accused former President Donald Trump of having knowledge of Epstein's financial dealings and sexua...

item.title: Colorado Governor Grants Clemency to Tina Peters, Reducing Sentence from 9 to 4.5 Years
item.description: Colorado Governor Jared Polis granted clemency to Tina Peters, the former Mesa County Clerk, reducing her prison sentence from nine years to four and a half years. Peters was convicted for tampering w...

item.title: Kiharu MP Ndindi Nyoro Proposes VAT Exemption and Levy Reductions to Ease Fuel Prices
item.description: Kiharu Member of Parliament Ndindi Nyoro has strongly urged the Kenyan government to take immediate action to reduce the rising cost of fuel. This comes after the latest review by the Energy and Petro...

item.title: Andy Burnham to seek Labour candidacy in Makerfield by-election
item.description: Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has received approval from Labour's National Executive Committee to seek selection as the party's candidate in the upcoming Makerfield by-election. Burnham had pr...

item.title: Five Italian Divers Perish in Maldives Cave Diving Accident, Investigation Underway
item.description: Five Italian divers, including respected marine biologist Monica Montefalcone and her daughter Giorgia Sommacal, tragically died while attempting to explore a deep underwater cave in the Maldives' Vaa...

item.title: CS Murkomen Implements Nationwide Reshuffle of Regional and County Security Administrators
item.description: Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen has carried out a major reshuffle affecting both regional and county security chiefs across Kenya. The changes, which take effect immediately, involve tra...

item.title: CAF eases skybox rules for Kasarani Stadium ahead of 2027 AFCON
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has agreed to relax some of its skybox requirements for the Moi International Sports Centre, Kasarani, allowing Kenya more flexibility as the country prepar...

item.title: Petite and Iyah Mina share behind-the-scenes highlights of “Everybody, Sing!”

item.title: Senators Introduce Bill to Permanently Ban Former Lawmakers from Lobbying
item.description: A bipartisan initiative is underway in the U.S. Senate to prevent former lawmakers from becoming lobbyists, a practice often criticized for undermining public trust. Republican Senator Rick Scott of F...

item.title: Strollio’s Luncheonette: Nostalgic 90s Bakery Bringing Artisan Bread to Tuart Hill
item.description: Strollio’s Luncheonette, which opened in mid-2024, has quickly become a standout bakery-café in Tuart Hill, Perth, thanks to its nostalgic 1990s vibe and expertly crafted breads. Co-owned by Hannah Bu...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Rod Stewart and Jeff Beck’s 1985 ‘People Get Ready’ Video in Oxnard
item.description: In May 1985, legendary musicians Rod Stewart and Jeff Beck reunited to film the music video for their cover of Curtis Mayfield’s classic song 'People Get Ready' in Oxnard, California. Photographer Jef...

item.title: Mumbai City FC defeats Mohammedan SC 4-0 to stay in ISL title contention
item.description: In a crucial Indian Super League match held at the Kishore Bharati Stadium in Kolkata on Friday, former champions Mumbai City FC delivered a strong performance by thrashing Mohammedan SC with a score ...

item.title: The Complex Science and Challenges of Sugar Alternatives
item.description: The article explores the evolving landscape of sweeteners, examining both their history and scientific complexities. Initially developed over a century ago to provide sugar-like sweetness without calo...

item.title: US and China Establish Trade and Investment Boards with Xi Jinping Invited to Washington
item.description: In a significant development in US-China relations, the two nations have agreed to establish trade and investment boards to manage their economic ties. This move comes alongside an invitation for Chin...

item.title: Parliament Appoints 31-Member Committee to Review Phala Phala Allegations Against President Ramaphosa
item.description: Parliament has appointed a 31-member impeachment committee following the Constitutional Court's ruling on the procedures related to the Phala Phala allegations against President Cyril Ramaphosa. This ...

item.title: Analyzing UCLA's 2027 Football Recruiting Class and Its Chances to Stay in the Top 10
item.description: UCLA's 2027 football recruiting class has drawn attention for being ranked in the top 10 nationally, an impressive feat for a program recovering from a disappointing 3-9 season. With 18 commits and an...

item.title: How France’s colonial past still shapes its relations with Africa today
item.description: The article talks about how France’s long history of empire and colonial rule continues to affect its ties with African countries, especially in the Sahel and former French‑speaking states. It explain...

item.title: Brown Mogotsi arrested over staged assassination attempt
item.description: Brown Mogotsi, a self-proclaimed crime intelligence agent, was arrested by a multidisciplinary SAPS team on Friday evening for allegedly defeating the ends of justice. The arrest is linked to a shooti...
